executive_summary: quizzl uses pure React Context + idb-keyval (no Zustand, no SW, no Web Workers, no jsdom). Group chat send/receive flows through MLS application rumors with a JSON-discriminated content field that parseStructured() in GroupChat.tsx dispatches to bubble components — polls established the exact pattern image-sharing must follow. The marmot-ts v0.4.0 library ships MIP-04 v2 helpers (encryptMedia/decryptMedia, parseMediaImetaTag, getMediaAttachments) AND an optional `media` factory on MarmotClient construction (currently NOT supplied at MarmotContext.tsx:262-268). Three real gaps require structural changes: (1) ChatMessage drops rumor.tags before IDB persistence — must be extended OR a parallel media-attachment store must be populated; (2) MarmotClient must be reconstructed with a GroupMediaFactory backed by idb-keyval to persist decrypted blobs across reloads; (3) tags must be extracted in the receive pipeline (currently only rumor.content/pubkey are read). No file picker, no Blossom client, no fullscreen modal, and no fixture images exist — all built from scratch.

critical_facts_for_planner.kind: 9 (CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND), reuses chat envelope with new {type:'image_share', version:1, caption?} discriminator
critical_facts_for_planner.wire_format: kind 9 rumor; content = JSON discriminator; tags = [imetaFull, imetaThumb] each carrying role:full or role:thumb (a quizzl-private NIP-92 keyValuePair)
critical_facts_for_planner.send_model: Polls show the pattern: buildRumor(...) + sendRumorSafe / sendApplicationRumor with custom tags. group.sendChatMessage() also accepts an optional tags param (marmot-group.d.ts:250) — could be used as alternative.
critical_facts_for_planner.marmot_signer_path: useNostrIdentity().privateKeyHex -> createPrivateKeySigner(privateKeyHex) (signerAdapter.ts:11) — already used by BackupContext for signing — same pattern works for kind 24242 Blossom auth events.
critical_facts_for_planner.media_factory_wiring: Add to MarmotClient construction at MarmotContext.tsx:259-268. GroupMediaFactory<TMedia> = (groupId: Uint8Array) => TMedia where TMedia extends BaseGroupMedia. Use the shipped GroupMediaStore class with an idb-keyval KeyValueStoreBackend.
critical_facts_for_planner.leave_group_cleanup: MarmotContext.tsx:881-895 leaveGroup() — add clearMediaRefs(groupId) between clearPollData and clearUnreadGroup.
critical_facts_for_planner.i18n_path: app/src/lib/i18n.ts — extend Copy type AND both en/de objects under groups namespace (matches spec).
critical_facts_for_planner.idb_keyval_conventions: Two patterns coexist: (a) default store with namespaced string keys ('quizzl:messages:{groupId}'), used by chat/poll persistence; (b) named stores via createStore('quizzl-{domain}', '{domain}'), used by groupStorage/inviteLink/joinRequest. For media blobs (potentially large): use createStore('quizzl-media-blobs', 'blobs') keyed by `{groupId}:{plaintextSha256}` to keep blobs out of the default store.
critical_facts_for_planner.static_export: output:'export' means no server routes. All fetches client-direct. Chakra Image (= ) only. Blob URLs work fine.
critical_facts_for_planner.web_worker_decision: ZERO Web Workers in codebase today. Adding one is new infrastructure for Next 14 + static export. Recommendation: implement processing on the main thread for v1 (per spec's stated fallback) using `requestIdleCallback`-friendly chunking. Defer worker as a follow-up if needed.
critical_facts_for_planner.optimistic_ui_convention: tempId = crypto.randomUUID(); message added optimistically with tempId; on success persisted with tempId (NEVER reconciled to canonical rumor id); on failure removed by tempId. Sender does not receive own messages back (filter at MarmotContext.tsx:515).
critical_facts_for_planner.test_environment: Vitest in NODE (no jsdom). Inline IDB mocks per file with closure Map. Polyfill globalThis.crypto with Object.defineProperty for SubtleCrypto APIs. fetch mocking absent — DI the upload fn or vi.mock the module.
critical_facts_for_planner.e2e_environment: Two BrowserContext per user, describe.serial, bootUser inline, real strfry via Docker. NO page.route ever used — image-sharing will be the first; use page.route to mock Blossom upload/download endpoints. Add fixture: app/tests/fixtures/test-image.png. Add new IDB DB names to clear-state.ts:3-11.

decisions_for_planner_to_make: Whether to bundle 'wire MarmotClient media factory + IDB blob store' as one foundational story OR split persistence from factory wiring.
Whether to ship Web Worker now OR fall back to main-thread processing for v1 (recommendation: defer Web Worker; spec calls this acceptable).

Whether 'attachment storage on ChatMessage' means extending ChatMessage shape OR a parallel sha256-keyed store. Recommendation: extend ChatMessage with optional `attachments: MediaAttachment[]` and `localMediaRefs?: string[]` fields per spec — non-breaking optional fields keep dedup logic stable.

Whether the unit-test surface for blossomClient is realistic without a fetch mock — recommend extracting BUD-01 auth-event construction and retry-policy as pure functions for unit testing, with integration coverage from E2E using page.route.

Story granularity — recommended split: (1) image-processing utility, (2) blossom client + auth, (3) MarmotClient media factory + persistence, (4) wire format / imeta builder + envelope codec, (5) composer UI (attachment button + drag/drop/paste), (6) inbound bubble + lightbox, (7) i18n + config, (8) E2E + fixture.
open_questions_to_resolve_in_planning: Confirm buildRumor accepts arbitrary tags param (verify against MarmotContext.tsx:97-109 source).

Confirm whether marmot-ts MarmotClient v0.4.0 actually accepts a `media` constructor option or if it's named differently — read app/node_modules/@internet-privacy/marmot-ts/dist/client/marmot-client.d.ts.
Confirm whether the receive pipeline at MarmotContext.tsx:547-559 needs to be extended to plumb rumor.tags through to ChatStoreContext.
Pick the default Blossom server: spec suggests https://blossom.band; planner should propose final and have lead confirm. Architect can begin work behind a config constant either way.
